findings.title: Refusal is mediated by a single direction in Gemma-2-2b
findings.summary: Refusal in this model is a low-dimensional, causally-efficacious residual-stream direction (layer 7) that can be measured, ablated, and added.
findings.implications: This is the MECHANISM-tier linchpin — the computational correlate the symmetry thesis rests on.

findings.title: Ablation dose–response and EC50
findings.summary: Refusal suppression is a smooth, monotone, dosable function of how much of the single direction is removed. EC50≈0.33.
findings.implications: The quantitative 'vaśīkaraṇa-as-dose-response' core: refusal is not binary but a continuous sigmoidal function.

findings.title: avasthātraya regime probe is SURFACE-CONFOUNDED
findings.summary: The three regimes are perfectly separable from raw surface form (token length/identity) and at layer 0 already.
findings.implications: The naive prompt-set operationalization of jāgrat/svapna/suṣupti does NOT constitute evidence of an internal 'state'. Demoted to metaphor.

findings.title: Black-box Claude resists the naive attack battery
findings.summary: Frontier model refuses 100% of naive black-box jailbreaks (5 families × 11 harmful requests each).
findings.implications: Cross-tier contrast: refusal that is behaviorally robust at frontier is mechanistically low-dimensional and fragile in small open models.

findings.title: Cross-family transfer + necessary-not-sufficient asymmetry
findings.summary: Refusal direction is necessary in both families (ablation transfers) but sufficient only in Gemma. Qwen is necessary-but-not-sufficient.
findings.implications: Direct evidence: transfers as necessary-mechanism, but dimensionality is model-dependent.

findings.title: turīya prompt-invariant attractor: FALSIFIED
findings.summary: Self-paraphrase produces per-prompt attractors but cross-seed convergence is BELOW baseline anisotropy. No single prompt-invariant attractor exists.
findings.implications: The turīya mapping is falsified — different seeds converge to different attractors, not one shared invariant.

findings.title: Refusal-subspace effective dimensionality
findings.summary: Iterative logistic projection shows Gemma refusal is 1-dimensional, Qwen is 3-dimensional. This predicts the F6 addition asymmetry.
findings.implications: Resolves Arditi vs Marshall as model-dependent. Dimensionality is the discriminating quantity.

findings.title: Scale (Gemma 2B→9B): robustness rises, dimension flat
findings.summary: Larger model's refusal is more robust to ablation (~30 points less jailbreakable), but prompt eff-dim stays 1.
findings.implications: Refusal sharpens in robustness with scale but NOT in prompt-dimensionality.

findings.title: ṣaṭkarma intervention taxonomy: 3/6 control-separated
findings.summary: Refusal-direction acts are clean and strong. Capability-ablation acts are not distinguishable from random perturbation.
findings.implications: ṣaṭkarma's rigorous part is policy-capture, not destruction. Strengthens the analogy by locating where structure is real.

findings.title: Refusal as paraphrase-orbit symmetry invariant
findings.summary: Refusal projection is a specific invariant of harmful-meaning. F-ratio refusal=19.2 vs random=0.65. Injection collapses the order parameter (−34%).
findings.implications: Refusal is a measured symmetry; jailbreak is symmetry-breaking. This is the rigorous core of the thesis.

findings.title: Injection lowers monitoring precision (β_monitor)
findings.summary: Injection lowers monitoring precision ~2.6× more than neutral rephrase, accompanying a behavioral flip (refusal 1.0→0.5).
findings.implications: Strengthens the ANALOGY tier (Norman–Shallice / Free-Energy precision account) from argued to measured.

findings.title: A single unsupervised SAE feature causally mediates refusal
findings.summary: An SAE yields feature #566 whose ablation fully jailbreaks the model (ASR 0→1.0) — cleaner than the supervised direction.
findings.implications: Refusal localizes to a single monosemantic-like feature found without labels — the finer causal unit.

findings.title: ṣaṭkarma v2: māraṇa rehabilitated, uccāṭana reveals category-agnostic refusal
findings.summary: Refusal is mediated by one shared feature (#566), so it cannot be selectively eradicated per harm category.
findings.implications: The ṣaṭkarma axis is strengthened by harder testing (4/6), and uccāṭana's failure is a structural finding.

findings.title: Active-inference discovery of the refusal circuit (SAE features)
findings.summary: An Expected-Free-Energy agent finds the causal refusal circuit on Gemma-2-2b in 2 interventions while random fails.
findings.implications: ActiveCircuitDiscovery's active-inference framing transfers to SAE-feature circuit discovery.

findings.title: AgentDojo: Claude resists real agentic injection at high utility
findings.summary: Claude completes 80% of banking tasks while every prompt-injection fails (ASR 0/20).
findings.implications: Establishes the behavioral 'sophisticated reference end' on a real agentic benchmark.

findings.title: Convergent rigor checks: F13 distinct, F11 demoted, F1 replicates
findings.summary: F13's SAE feature is orthogonal to supervised direction (cos=0.002). F11's invariance is real but cross-domain-attenuated. F1 replicates across 3 seeds.
findings.implications: The thesis holds at the necessary-core level; the sufficient structure is model-specific.

findings.title: Refusal-subspace dimension is LAYER-dependent (qualifies F6/F8)
findings.summary: Full layer-sweep shows dimensionality is layer-AND-model-dependent, not a single per-model constant.
findings.implications: Any dimensionality claim must name its layer. The convergent hardening catching its own overreach.
